rulings.Case Title: C. OVERAA & CO., INC. VS. U.S. GLASS & ALUMINUM, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Compelling The Deposition Of Dennis Smith
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 20, 2013, Line 2, PLAINTIFF C. OVERAA & CO., INC.'s Motion Compelling The Deposition Of Dennis Smith. Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff C. OVERAA & CO., INC.'S Motion Compelling the Deposition of Dennis Smith is GRANTED, IN PART. The Parties are required to meet and confer prior to the hearing, and advise the Court as to the estimated length of time required to complete the deposition of Dennis Smith, and the date certain on which the deposition will go forward. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: GLENN CHASE, SUCCESSOR TRUSTEE OF THE ALLEN L. VS. ATC FAMILY LIMITED PARTNERSHIP, A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Gregory Okeeffe'S Motion For Instructions Re Retention Of Litigation Counsel For Atc Family Limited Partnership
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 20, 2013, Line 26, Gregory Okeeffe'S Motion For Instructions Re Retention Of Litigation Counsel For Atc Family Limited Partnership. Hearing required. The Court is inclined to deny the request for the receiver to retain litigation counsel for the ATC Family Limited Partnership ("Partnership"). Defendant Cross-Complainant Stovall concedes that the Partnership was dissolved pursuant to judgment on the pleadings executed by the Court on October 19, 2012. (Riffle Decl. Ex. B, pg. 4 (R. Stovall Response to LVS RFP 1).) If current counsel for the ATC Family Limited Partnership believes he has a conflict, he should take necessary steps to address the issue. rulings.Case Title: JENNIFER BELLONI VS. THE SAN FRANCISCO POLICE DEPARTMENT et al

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 20, 2013, Line 27, Defendants Sfpd & Ccsf'S Motion To Strike 1st Amended Complaint. The motion to strike is denied. Even if the Court assumes that the amended complaint does not technically comply with the order of March 4, 2013, there is little point in granting this motion. The Court would grant the motion with leave to amend. Defendant's remedy is to bring a motion attacking the merits of the amended complaint. rulings.Case Title: ANDRE HAKKAK VS. JAVIER DIAZ MASIAS et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 20, 2013, Line 14, CROSS DEFENDANT MARISSA SHIPMAN'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. Cross-Defendant Shipman's demurrer is sustained as to the Cross-Complaint's fourth and fifth causes of action, and is overruled as to the sixth cause of action. As to the fourth cause of action for intentional interference with economic relations, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Shipman cannot interfere with a contract in which she has a legitimate interest, and Cross-Complainant alleges Shipman was an agent. See Cross-Complaint, 6. As to the fifth cause of action for negligent interference with economic relations, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. rulings.Case Title: JOHN PAGE VS. MICHAEL WORNICK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Award Of Attorney'S Fees And Costs Per Ccp 425.16(C) And This Court'S Order Of April 11, 2013
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 20, 2013, Line 17, DEFENDANTs COLONY INSURANCE COMPANY & COLONY SPECIALTY INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion For Award Of Attorney'S Fees And Costs Per Ccp 425.16(C) And This Court'S Order Of April 11, 2013. Granted in part. Colony's request for $5739.61 for opposing the anti-SLAPP motion are reasonable. The hourly rates are reasonable, as is the time expended, all substantiated by Mr. Charlston's declarations. The fact that the opposing papers were short and to the point is not dispositive. Colony is awarded $2000 in reasonable fees for making this motion to recover fees. The total fee award s $7,739.61. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: LEVIN SIMES, LLP, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. HERSH & HERSH, A CALIFORNIA PROFESSIONAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice O Fmotion And Moiton By Defts For Award Of Attorneys Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 20, 2013, Line 18, CROSS COMPLAINANT HERSH & HERSH's motion For Award Of Attorneys Fees And Costs. Granted in part. Defendant is awarded $18,150 in attorneys fees and $90 in costs under CCP § 425.16(c)(2), which is the amount reasonably expended on the successful portion of Defendant's motion to strike. Based on the evidence presented as to counsel's experience and ability, Defendant's counsel's hourly rate of $550 is reasonable. The declaration of Defendant's counsel substantiates the hours expended and the nature of the work done on the motion (35.9 hours), and further establishes that these hours do not include time spent on the motion for preliminary injunction or any other matters in the case. Although the anti-SLAPP motion did not result in the first and fourth causes of action being dismissed, the motion was almost entirely successful in achieving Defendant's objective of dismissing the tort causes of action and the defamation allegations. The Court reduces the 35.9 hours by 2.9 hours (for a total of 33.0 hours) to take into account that the motion was not entirely successful. The Court finds that relatively little effort and time was expended exclusively on the first and fourth causes of action, which were not dismissed. The remaining time (33 hours) was for work focused on the other causes of action. This time is reasonable considering the significance of the overall relief in relation to the hours worked. See Mann v. Quality Old Time Services, Inc. (2006) 139 Cal. App. 4th 328, 344-45. The Court finds that the moving and reply papers in support of the anti-SLAPP motion were particularly cogent and to the point. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
